title: Vector DB Health Checklist
concept: A practical pre-launch checklist covering recall testing, backup strategy, and monitoring setup.

exercises.prompt: From our last lesson, which of these is a common mistake when setting up a vector database?

options.text: Scaling up the hardware before measuring actual data usage.
Using a different distance metric than the one used to train your embedding model.
Creating an index before adding any data to the database.

exercises.explain: Matching the distance metric (like Cosine Similarity) used during model training with the metric configured in your database is critical for accurate search results.

cards.title: Is Your Vector DB Ready?
cards.body: Imagine your AI app goes viral overnight, but suddenly starts returning completely irrelevant results, or crashes due to running out of memory. How do we prevent these launch-day nightmares?

cards.title: What is a Health Checklist?
cards.body: A vector DB health checklist is a set of mandatory validation steps—specifically recall testing, automated backups, and real-time monitoring—run before launching to production to ensure search quality and system stability.

cards.title: The Pre-Flight Control Panel
cards.body: Think of launching your vector database like launching a rocket. You wouldn't launch without checking the fuel (backup), testing the thrusters (recall), and watching the control panel (monitoring).

cards.title: Checklist in Action
cards.body: Before launching an e-commerce semantic search, a team runs automated tests comparing vector search results against a human-curated golden dataset (recall test), schedules daily snapshots to AWS S3, and sets up alerts for RAM spikes.

cards.title: The 'Set and Forget' Myth
cards.body: Many developers assume vector databases are 'set it and forget it.' In reality, index performance and accuracy degrade as new data is added, requiring active monitoring and tuning.

cards.title: Safe Launch vs. Blind Launch
cards.body: Compare a healthy, production-ready setup with an unmonitored launch disaster.

left.text: Recall tested at 92%, daily automated backups scheduled, RAM alerts active.

right.text: No baseline test, zero backups, crashes silently when memory runs out.

exercises.prompt: Which metric directly measures the quality and relevance of your vector database's search results before launch?

options.text: Search recall accuracy against a golden dataset
The size of the backup files
The number of dimensions in your embeddings

exercises.explain: Recall measures the quality and accuracy of the search results, ensuring the vector DB actually finds the most similar items. Memory and disk space monitor system resources, not search accuracy.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A robust backup strategy is necessary because rebuilding a massive vector index from raw data can take a long time and disrupt services.
exercises.explain: Backups are critical because vector indexes can become corrupted during crashes, and reconstructing them from scratch can take hours or days of heavy GPU processing.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing practice that verifies search accuracy against a ground-truth dataset.
exercises.sentence: We use recall testing to measure if our vector database is returning the correct items compared to a golden dataset.
exercises.explain: Recall testing compares your vector database's actual search results against a known correct 'golden dataset' to measure real-world retrieval accuracy.

exercises.prompt: Identify the system-generated alert message from a production vector monitoring tool.

options.text: 'I think we should back up our vector database tonight just to be safe.'
'I am going to write a script to test if the database is running.'
'[ALERT] Search recall has dropped below 85% threshold on production index "products_v2".'

exercises.explain: Option C mimics a real-world warning message generated by an automated monitoring tool alerting engineers of degraded vector search quality. The other options are general developer statements.

exercises.prompt: You are preparing to launch a vector-powered search engine. Which of the following represents a complete, production-ready health checklist?

options.text: Only monitoring search latency and memory usage daily.
Writing a script to measure index creation speed every hour.
Verifying search recall accuracy, scheduling automated backups, and setting up RAM/latency alerts.
Increasing the embedding dimension size right before launch.

exercises.explain: A complete health plan requires validating quality (recall tests), preparing for disaster (automated backups), and watching live performance (latency/RAM monitoring). Measuring index creation time is helpful during development but isn't a critical production health metric.
